title: How Ritsuko typed in Maya laptop
display_title: How Ritsuko typed in Maya laptop
wikitext:
[]: How Ritsuko typed in Maya laptop             Quote Post by Barinax » Sun Oct 07, 2007 4:26 pm And I the only one who thought she typed in "ineedyuo"? Or is her typing just really fast?

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sun Oct 07, 2007 4:28 pm She programs computers as part of her job. I think she probably types fast. Also, keep in mind that we aren't REALLY seeing "Ritsuko".

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Oct 07, 2007 4:29 pm I actually went through that frame by frame a while back, and she does indeed type "yuo". If you want to fanwank around it, you could say that the word processor Maya was using automatically corrected it (as some word processors do).

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sun Oct 07, 2007 4:34 pm I think it's engrish.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Sun Oct 07, 2007 4:34 pm Me thinks it's just a case of "Engrish" that got overlooked. EDIT: Chee, my post was longer, so I get credit for the 1st Engrish comment. Kay?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Oct 07, 2007 5:31 pm No, no: the output on the laptop actually said "I NEED YOU", but if you look at what keys Ritsuko's fingers press, she types "i need yuo".

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sun Oct 07, 2007 5:53 pm TriLink12 wrote: Me thinks it's just a case of "Engrish" that got overlooked. EDIT: Chee, my post was longer, so I get credit for the 1st Engrish comment. Kay?

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Sun Oct 07, 2007 7:01 pm NAveryW wrote: No, no: the output on the laptop actually said "I NEED YOU", but if you look at what keys Ritsuko's fingers press, she types "i need yuo".

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun Oct 07, 2007 7:15 pm My biggest admirer in this forum (TriLink12) wrote: NAveryW-kun wrote: No, no: the output on the laptop actually said "I NEED YOU", but if you look at what keys Ritsuko's fingers press, she types "i need yuo".

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Oct 07, 2007 7:24 pm My biggest admirer in this forum (TriLink12) wrote: NAveryW-kun wrote: No, no: the output on the laptop actually said "I NEED YOU", but if you look at what keys Ritsuko's fingers press, she types "i need yuo".

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Oct 07, 2007 7:32 pm My biggest admirer in this forum (TriLink12) wrote: NAveryW-kun wrote: No, no: the output on the laptop actually said "I NEED YOU", but if you look at what keys Ritsuko's fingers press, she types "i need yuo".

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sun Oct 07, 2007 7:50 pm instrumentally had started Im pretty sure anything could have happened at that point.

[]:             Quote Post by Apathetic Piggy » Sun Oct 07, 2007 7:57 pm BrikHaus wrote: My biggest admirer in this forum (TriLink12) wrote: NAveryW-kun wrote: No, no: the output on the laptop actually said "I NEED YOU", but if you look at what keys Ritsuko's fingers press, she types "i need yuo".

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Oct 07, 2007 8:54 pm Do I have a life? Perhaps not. Do I need one? Definitely not! If you have no life, you can't die! =D That last part was kind of paraphrased from South Park , I suppose...

[]:             Quote Post by Barinax » Sun Oct 07, 2007 9:19 pm Having a life is somewhat overrated...

[]:             Quote Post by Jay911 » Sun Oct 07, 2007 11:36 pm INEEDYUO? Was Ritsuko the last 4channer?

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Mon Oct 08, 2007 12:46 am The answer to this dilemma in one word: Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Mon Oct 08, 2007 12:43 pm Ironfoot wrote: The answer to this dilemma in one word: Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Oct 08, 2007 1:49 pm Jay911 wrote: INEEDYUO? Was Ritsuko the last 4channer?

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Oct 08, 2007 3:57 pm oh noes!
